title: OMH Plugin: Critique-and-fix loop until no P1/P2 issues
description: Run parallel critique team (security, architecture, code quality) on the plugin codebase. Fix all P1 (Critical) and P2 (High) issues found. Repeat until a fresh critique pass raises zero P1 or P2 findings.

stories.title: Run initial critique team and catalogue all findings
stories.description: Spawn parallel security, architect, and code-reviewer agents against the plugin codebase. Collect all findings, classify each by severity (P1=Critical, P2=High, P3=Medium, P4=Low), and write findings to .omc/critique-findings.json.
acceptanceCriteria: .omc/critique-findings.json exists and contains a structured list of findings with id, severity, file, description, and remediation fields
All three review perspectives (security, architecture, code-quality) are represented in the findings
Each finding has severity classified as P1, P2, P3, or P4

stories.title: Fix all P1 and P2 findings from the critique
stories.description: For each finding in .omc/critique-findings.json with severity P1 or P2, implement the remediation. Update the finding's 'fixed: true' field when done. All 82 tests must still pass after fixes.
acceptanceCriteria: Every finding with severity P1 or P2 in critique-findings.json has fixed: true
python3 -m pytest plugin/tests/ -q exits with code 0 and shows 0 failures
No new P1 or P2 issues introduced by the fixes (verified by re-reading changed files)

stories.title: Re-run critique team and confirm no P1/P2 issues remain
stories.description: Run a fresh parallel critique pass (security + architect + code-reviewer) on the updated codebase. Confirm zero P1 or P2 findings. P3/P4 findings are acceptable.
acceptanceCriteria: Fresh critique pass completes with all three reviewers
Zero P1 (Critical) findings reported by any reviewer
Zero P2 (High) findings reported by any reviewer
python3 -m pytest plugin/tests/ -q exits with code 0
